UPMC is laying off 200 employees and eliminating 300 open positions, primarily in non-clinical roles. The cuts follow previous pandemic-related reductions and occur despite recent facility expansions and financial gains.

external.description:
June 10, 2026, 9:49 a.m. ET

UPMC is laying off 200 employees and eliminating 300 open positions, primarily in non-clinical roles.

This follows a previous layoff of 1,000 employees two years ago due to post-pandemic challenges.

The cuts come despite recent financial gains and expansion, including a new heart institute.

Just two years after they laid off 1,000 employees because of "post-pandemic challenges," UPMC is cutting 500 more positions, according to multiple reports.

The hospital network and insurance provider, which is the largest nongovernment employer in Pennsylvania, announced 200 employee layoffs on June 9, the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette reported. UPMC will also cut 300 additional open positions.

Here's what to know about the staffing cuts.

What kind of positions did UPMC eliminate in layoffs?

UPMC's layoffs are taking place "primarily among those who are not in clinical or member-facing roles," UPMC said in a statement.

This isn't the first time UPMC staff has been reduced in recent years. Layoffs also took place in 2024, when UPMC cut more than 1,000 employees due to "post-pandemic challenges," TribLIVE reported.

The healthcare provider and insurer is the largest nongovernment employer in Pennsylvania, with about 100,000 employees, according to UPMC's website. Of those employees, 5,000 are doctors.

The network has 40 hospitals and 800 outpatient sites in Pennsylvania, New York and Maryland, and it's growing: In May, UPMC acquired Trinity Health System, a hospital network based in eastern Ohio, and this month, the network opened its $85 million heart institute expansion at UPMC Children's Hospital in Lawrenceville, TribLIVE reported.

Finances fluctuate at UPMC over recent years

UPMC's finances have fluctuated over the past few years as the healthcare network suffered operating losses of $339 million in 2024 and $198 million in 2023, TribLIVE reported. The network bounced back in 2025 when its operating income hit $286 million. Part of the increase in income came in the insurance services department because of higher income from Medicaid.

Even still, in May, nonprofit consumer advocacy organization Public Citizen released a report flagging three UPMC hospitals in southwest Pennsylvania as at risk of closure because of legislation cutting Medicaid spending — UPMC Mercy, UPMC McKeesport and UPMC Greene.

Healthcare workers call on UPMC to invest in staff

Michelle Hart, a neonatal nurse practitioner, said in a statement from the union representing workers at UPMC Magee-Womens Hospital that UPMC must "invest in, rather than reduce, our staff," TribLIVE reported.

"UPMC cannot continue to prioritize branding, construction and executive compensation over investing in frontline staff," Hart said.

SEIU Healthcare Pennsylvania is in the process of negotiating a contract for nurses and advanced practitioners at Magee, the Post-Gazette reported.

record.text: East Cocalico Township is updating spa and massage parlor regulations to align with countywide efforts to combat human trafficking. The proposed ordinance will require all new facilities to be fully licensed.

external.description:
When: East Cocalico supervisors meeting, June 4.

What happened: In response to a letter from the Lancaster County District Attorney’s office, the supervisors voted to advertise an ordinance redefining the use of spas and massage parlors.

Background: Lorenzo Bonura, board chair, said the ordinance is directed toward “a certain definition that relates to adult uses.” The ordinance, expected to be voted on at the board’s July 8 meeting, will bring the township into line with countywide with efforts against human trafficking. Any “spa” already in business, Bonura noted, would be immune from the new ordinance.

Quotable: “If they are currently operating as a ‘spa,’ there’s no way we can send the zoning officer there and say they are not in compliance with the new zoning,” he said.

Licensing required: Spas and massage facilities seeking to operate in the township must be fully licensed.

Street cut moratorium: The board is planning to increase the time between resurfacing a street and when an outside business may cut into the surface to place cables and other underground services. The interval would go from four years to six years once the ordinance is approved. The ordinance is currently in draft form but could possibly be voted on later this month.

Compost hours: Summer hours for the township’s compost site behind the township office will be 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. Saturday, and 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. Sunday.

Police chief retained: The board announced a letter of agreement with police Chief Steve Savage for him to continue in his role at least two more years. The new agreement takes effect Jan. 1. His salary remains $141,255 as budgeted for this year.

Quotables: “We’re very happy to have Chief Savage,” Bonura said. “I hear nothing but good things.” After the meeting Savage said, “I’m blessed and proud to be here and serve the residents of East Cocalico and Denver Borough. I couldn’t be happier.”

Next up: The supervisors will next meet at 7 p.m. June 18 at the township office, 100 Hill Road, Denver.
external.title: East Cocalico Twp. to tweak spa regulations to conform with county efforts against human trafficking

Der Anime entsteht bei P.A.WORKS unter der Regie von Osamu Honma (Ya Boy Kongming!, Mayonaka Punch). Die Serienkomposition übernimmt Yoko Yonaiyama (A Sign of Affection, Akiba Maid War), während das Charakterdesign und die Chief Animation Direction von Yuito Kiyosawa stammen. Für das Gag- und Sub-Charakterdesign sowie ebenfalls die Chief Animation Direction ist Masami Goda verantwortlich.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Als Ausrüstungsverzauberer Rein plötzlich aus seiner Gilde geworfen wird, ist der Grund fast schon lächerlich: Alle Waffen und Rüstungen wurden bereits genug verstärkt. Wütend zieht er die gewaltige Menge Mana zurück, die er über lange Zeit in die Ausrüstung der Gilde gesteckt hat. Als er diese Kraft testweise in ein einfaches Kupferschwert leitet, entsteht daraus eine völlig übermächtige Waffe mit 10.000 Verstärkungspunkten. Ohne Kampferfahrung, aber mit einer Magie, die selbst gewöhnliche Ausrüstung in Cheat-Gear verwandelt, beginnt für Rein ein neues Leben als Abenteurer.

record.text:
(早上穿了深v襯衫+灰色包臀裙準備上班)

老公：(瞥一眼)你穿這樣…要是在公司被人多看幾眼，晚上的懲罰會更重。
我：才不會有人看我…
老公：下午開完會記得把外套穿上。
我：才沒人敢看我勒。(嘴硬，準備出門)

(突然被老公抱上流理台，強迫分開雙腿，他卡在中間）

老公：該讓你長長記性…

(接著他就在我側頸吸出一個吻痕)

我：欸，這裡很明顯，會被別人看見啦…
老公：所以你終於承認會有人看你？

```
jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: codeberg-tiny
```

What: Defines a job named "publish" and the environment it runs on.

The Difference (codeberg-tiny vs ubuntu-latest):
* codeberg-tiny: This is Codeberg's specific, lightweight runner. It is faster to boot up for simple tasks, uses less resources, and is free specifically for Codeberg users. It usually comes pre-installed with basic tools.
* ubuntu-latest: This is the standard GitHub Runner image. It is massive, has almost every tool installed, but takes longer to start. On Codeberg, using ubuntu-latest might work, but codeberg-tiny is the native, optimized choice.

Verdict: Stick with codeberg-tiny. It's faster and more efficient for static sites.

Chat mit ChatGPT:

Frage: Was bedeutet das Zeichen zwischen a und e bei Stra车enbahn?

Antwort: Das Zeichen zwischen „a“ und „e“ bei Straßenbahnen ist ein Umlautzeichen: ä. Es steht für den Lautwechsel in vielen deutschen Wörtern (z. B. Bahnhäuser, Straße). Also „Straßenbahn“ hat den Umlaut ä.

Frage: An welcher Stelle steht das ä im Wort Straßenbahn?

Antwort: Das „ä“ steht in dem Wort Straßenbahn an der zweiten Silbe: Straße-nbahn. Der Vokalwechsel erfolgt nach dem Umlaut „ä“ in „Straße“.
